questions.explanation: A quadratic equation has degree 2. The standard form is ax² + bx + c = 0 where a ≠ 0.

questions.text: A quadratic equation can have at most 2 roots.
questions.explanation: A quadratic equation can have at most 2 roots (solutions). These can be real and distinct, real and equal, or complex.

questions.text: What is the discriminant of a quadratic equation?
questions.explanation: Discriminant D = b² - 4ac determines the nature of roots: D > 0 (real distinct), D = 0 (real equal), D < 0 (complex).
